method: inventory every Kidde PDF and its existing S99 retriever-augmentation coverage
exclude every source identity appearing anywhere in S203 through S209 packets, including secondary source arrays and citations
exclude every source identity appearing in the official gold ledger
prefer natural product-selection or architectural comparisons that require evidence from multiple documents
freeze source-page geometry before any Frontier authorship or chatbot output
defer semantic novelty, question adequacy and gold correctness to independent Sol and Fable pixel authorship plus reciprocal cross-review

selected.reason: The three datasheets expose a natural family-selection predicate across zone count, network-node geometry, power supply, supervised outputs and battery capacity; same-source S99 asks isolated specifications but not this bounded cross-model decision.

The pair supports a bounded selection predicate around no-Fire-Brigade versus integrated Scandinavian interface, key-lock consequences and supervised-output geometry. Loop cardinality is explicitly excluded because the AF1 sheet contradicts itself.

The sheets support a precise purchasing and installation distinction: only the KIT sheet explicitly says it is supplied complete with a back box, while both share the bounded MCP capabilities shown in their own pixels.
The pair supports a clean architectural predicate separating the Standard display module user-interface/configuration role from the detector module aspiration, analysis, relay and sampling-pipe role.

rejected_after_visual_inspection.reason: KE-AS3115R-WM occurs as a secondary source in S204; the pair is therefore not fresh even though a shallow source_pdf-only inventory had missed it. Its rendered pages were removed before packet freeze.
The AF1 title/details say one loop while its narrative and technical table say two; authors must avoid loop cardinality rather than silently resolve the conflict.

claims_not_made: exhaustive proof that no other useful Kidde predicate exists
